Package component.modifier.pathway

Class Entrance

java.lang.Object
component.modifier.Modifier
component.modifier.pathway.Pathway
component.modifier.pathway.Entrance
All Implemented Interfaces:
Tippable, GridIndexable
public class Entrance extends Pathway
The Entrance class is a specialized Pathway that handles the spawning of cards at the start of a level's logic loop.

It acts as the primary source of cards, pulling from the predefined input queue and emitting CardEnterEvent signals to notify the rest of the system that a new card has entered the board.

  • Constructor Details

    • Entrance

      public Entrance()

  • Method Details

    • modify

      public void modify(Card toModify)
      Attempts to spawn the next card from the level's input queue.

      Logic sequence: 1. Fetches the current card from the GameLevel.INPUT_CARDS list. 2. If a card is available, it tries to place it on this tile's coordinates. 3. Upon success, it emits a CardEnterEvent via the EventBus. 4. Increments the currentIndex to prepare for the next spawn cycle. * @param toModify The card currently occupying the tile (ignored by this specific logic).

      Specified by:
      modify in class Modifier
      Parameters:
      toModify - The card currently interacting with the tile.

    • isBlocking

      public boolean isBlocking()
      Specified by:
      isBlocking in interface GridIndexable
      Overrides:
      isBlocking in class Pathway
      Returns:
      false. Entrances must never block movement, as cards need to be able to move away from the spawn point.

    • getTooltip

      public Tooltip getTooltip()
      Generates a tooltip that previews the next card to be spawned.

      This provides crucial information to the player, allowing them to anticipate which card value or suit is coming next. * @return A Tooltip detailing the entrance state.

      Specified by:
      getTooltip in interface Tippable
      Overrides:
      getTooltip in class Modifier
      Returns:
      A Tooltip object with descriptive text and theme colors.
